title: Community Shelters * Homeless Couples - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Community Shelters * Homeless Couples 4 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Ex-Offenders Homeless Couples Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Homeless Youth Mothers Pregnant Individuals Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF DO GOOD MULTNOMAH ROSEWAY MOTEL SHELTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a reservation based motel shelter. Eligibility: Age 18 and older, individuals, and couples Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter locator with bed availability in Lane County. Not all shelters in Lane County are listed. 151 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by shelter Hours: Not applicable (541) 682-3798 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS LAURELWOOD CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shelter and service center for up to 120 people experiencing homelessness. The site has a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day and provides onsite meals. Staff will provide housing-focused support, employment and benefits assistance, and health-focused services. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 6130 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older or couples (any two people who present as a couple, regardless of gender) who are experiencing homelessness. Priority is given to people age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS WILLAMETTE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shelter and service center for up to 120 people experiencing homelessness. The site has 2 large sleeping rooms, a space for coffee and tea, books and board games, a gated courtyard, showers and laundry, space to leave belongings during the day, and a kitchen for light food and beverages. Staff will provide information, referral, and support. Onsite support services include housing, health, and income resources. Beds at a shelter are not guaranteed. 5120 SE Milwaukie Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older or couples (any two people who present as a couple, regardless of gender). Priority is given to people age 55 and older, adults with disabilities, and veterans. Pets allowed; must be housebroken, and the owner is responsible for caring for, feeding, and cleaning up after the animal Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Resource Center (in person or phone): Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays 8:30am-3pm; service intakes end 15 minutes before close (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER TRANSITION PROJECTS LAURELWOOD CENTER TRANSITION PROJECTS WILLAMETTE CENTER
